summary: Dan Shapiro announces his return to blogging after a months-long absence caused by secretly founding a new company, Ontela Inc., while maintaining his day job. He explains he couldn't discuss the venture publicly while still employed elsewhere. As of the post date, he's transitioning to work at Ontela full-time, which he hopes will free up small amounts of time for his woodworking hobby and blog.
key_points: Shapiro founded Ontela Inc. with a friend while still employed at his day job, consuming all his spare time
He kept the venture secret from blog readers because discussing a side company while employed is considered poor form
He's transitioning to full-time work at the new startup, expecting slightly more personal time with one job instead of two
money_quotes: Several months ago I founded a company with a friend.
I will be working my butt off at just one job instead of two, leaving dozens of minutes a week for woodworking and blogging and personal hygene.
key_insight: An early glimpse of Dan Shapiro's entrepreneurial trajectory, as he leaves his day job to go full-time on his startup Ontela—a move that would become a recurring pattern in his career.
